steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Set up the volume formula for the original cylinder
steps.content: For cylinder A with volume 28 cubic centimeters and radius $r_A$ and height $h_A$, we have $\pi r_A^2 h_A = 28$.

steps.title: Apply the new dimensions and calculate
steps.content:
The new cylinder has radius $2r_A$ (twice the radius) and height $\frac{1}{2}h_A$ (half the height). Substituting into the volume formula:
$$V_{\text{new}} = \pi(2r_A)^2 \left(\frac{1}{2}h_A\right)$$
$$= \pi \cdot 4r_A^2 \cdot \frac{1}{2}h_A$$
$$= 2\pi r_A^2 h_A$$
$$= 2 \cdot 28 = 56$$
That confirms C is correct.
